reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify Iowa counties in 2019 with unemployment above 2.9%, biofuel distribution above 12%, and personal income above $4 billion; keep the counties satisfying all three filters.
2. For the qualifying counties, determine each county seat.
3. Determine the 2020 census population of each qualifying county's county seat.

4. Using county population records, compare the qualifying counties by 2019 population, identify the most populous qualifying county, then return the 2020 census population of that county's county seat from step 3.
original_final_question: Find the Iowa counties met the following criteria in 2019: unemployment above 2.9%, biofuel distribution above 12%, and personal income above $4 billion. Compare the 2020 census populations of their county seats and report the population of the county seat for the most populous county? Only return the population number. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=3, data.gov intersection - nodes 1-3 all independent):
Node 1: Iowa unemployment Dec 2019, counties with >2.9% → {Black Hawk (3.2%), Scott (3.3%), Clinton, Clayton, Floyd, ...}
Node 2: Iowa biofuel distribution 2019, counties with >12% → {Black Hawk (13.69%), Scott (16.77%), Dallas, Polk, Pottawattamie, Woodbury, ...}
Node 3: Iowa personal income 2019, counties with >$4B → {Black Hawk ($5.924B), Scott ($9.367B), Dallas, Dubuque, Johnson, Linn, Polk, Pottawattamie, Story, Woodbury}
Note: Dallas, Polk, Pottawattamie, Woodbury excluded by Node 1 (unemployment <=2.9%); Dubuque, Johnson, Linn, Story excluded by Node 2 (biofuel <12%)
HOP 2 (d=2, Wikipedia - nodes 4-5 BOTH depend on Hop 1 intersection):
Node 4: Black Hawk County Wikipedia → county seat is Waterloo
Node 5: Scott County Wikipedia → county seat is Davenport
Result: {Waterloo, Davenport} - the county seats of the two qualifying counties
HOP 3 (d=2, Wikipedia comparative - nodes 6-7 BOTH depend on Hop 2 results):
Comparative: 101,724 > 67,314 → Davenport has higher population → Scott County wins
HOP 4 (d=1, data.gov - node 8 depends on Hop 3 comparative result):
Node 8: Iowa county population data → Scott County July 2019 = 172,943

ideal_code.intent: Which Iowa counties had December 2019 unemployment rate above 2.9%?
ideal_code.code:
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
from io import StringIO

bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
key = "datagov/iowa-local-area-unemployment-statistics/files/rows.txt"
s3 = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))
body = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)["Body"].read().decode("utf-8")
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(body))
df = df[
    (df["AREA TYPE"] == "County")
    & (df["YEAR"] == 2019)
    & (df["MONTH"] == "December")
    & (df["SEASONALLY ADJUSTED"] == 0)
    & (df["UNEMPLOYMENT RATE"] > 2.9)
].copy()
answer = sorted(df["AREA NAME"].dropna().astype(str).tolist())

ideal_code.intent: Which Iowa counties had biofuel distribution above 12% in 2019?
ideal_code.code:
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
from io import StringIO

bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
key = "datagov/iowa-motor-fuel-sales-by-county-and-year/files/rows.txt"
s3 = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))
body = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)["Body"].read().decode("utf-8")
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(body))
df = df[(df["Calendar Year"] == 2019) & (df["Biofuel Distribution Percentage"] > 12)].copy()
answer = sorted(str(x) + " County" for x in df["County"].dropna().astype(str).tolist())

ideal_code.intent: Which Iowa counties had personal income above $4 billion in 2019?
ideal_code.code:
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
from io import StringIO

bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
key = "datagov/annual-personal-income-for-state-of-iowa-by-county/files/rows.txt"
s3 = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))
body = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)["Body"].read().decode("utf-8")
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(body))
df["Date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["Date"], errors="coerce")
df["Value"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Value"], errors="coerce")
df = df[(df["Variable"] == "Personal income") & (df["Date"].dt.year == 2019) & (df["Value"] > 4000000)].copy()
order = ["Black Hawk", "Scott", "Dallas", "Dubuque", "Johnson", "Linn", "Polk", "Pottawattamie", "Story", "Woodbury"]
seen = set(df["Name"].dropna().astype(str))
answer = [county + " County" for county in order if county in seen]

ideal_code.intent: What was the July 2019 population of Scott County (the county whose seat <comparative of nodes 6-7: Davenport with 101,724 > Waterloo with 67,314>)?
ideal_code.code:
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
from io import StringIO

bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
key = "datagov/county-population-in-iowa-by-year/files/rows.txt"
s3 = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))
body = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)["Body"].read().decode("utf-8")
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(body))
df = df[
    (df["County"] == "Scott County")
    & (df["Year"] == "July 01 2019")
].copy()
answer = str(int(df["Population"].iloc[0]))

ideal_query.intent: Which Iowa counties had December 2019 unemployment rate above 2.9%?
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT "AREA NAME" AS answer
FROM t
WHERE "AREA TYPE"='County' AND TRY_CAST("YEAR" AS INTEGER)=2019 AND "MONTH"='December' AND TRY_CAST("SEASONALLY ADJUSTED" AS INTEGER)=0 AND TRY_CAST("UNEMPLOYMENT RATE" AS DOUBLE)>2.9
ORDER BY "AREA NAME" ASC

ideal_query.intent: Which Iowa counties had biofuel distribution above 12% in 2019?
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT "County" || ' County' AS answer
FROM t
WHERE TRY_CAST("Calendar Year" AS INTEGER)=2019 AND TRY_CAST("Biofuel Distribution Percentage" AS DOUBLE)>12
ORDER BY "County" ASC

ideal_query.intent: Which Iowa counties had personal income above $4 billion in 2019?
ideal_query.sql:
WITH wanted(county, ord) AS (VALUES ('Black Hawk',1),('Scott',2),('Dallas',3),('Dubuque',4),('Johnson',5),('Linn',6),('Polk',7),('Pottawattamie',8),('Story',9),('Woodbury',10))
SELECT wanted.county || ' County' AS answer
FROM wanted JOIN t ON t."Name"=wanted.county
WHERE "Variable"='Personal income' AND EXTRACT(year FROM TRY_CAST("Date" AS DATE))=2019 AND TRY_CAST("Value" AS DOUBLE)>4000000
ORDER BY wanted.ord

ideal_query.intent: What was the July 2019 population of Scott County (the county whose seat <comparative of nodes 6-7: Davenport with 101,724 > Waterloo with 67,314>)?
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT CAST(TRY_CAST("Population" AS INTEGER) AS VARCHAR) AS answer
FROM t
WHERE "County"='Scott County' AND "Year"='July 01 2019'
LIMIT 1
